steps.title: Capture skepticism and outcome
steps.content: The passage says critics warned that writing in Gĩkũyũ might limit Ngũgĩ's reach, but he later wrote acclaimed works translated into more than thirty languages. The last sentence overturns the warning by showing broad reception outside his community despite skepticism.

steps.title: Reject distorted summaries
steps.content: A wrongly treats Gĩkũyũ as widely understood, B says his first works were in Gĩkũyũ, and D invents a belief about all authors. The main idea is skepticism followed by success, not one biographical motive or the chronology of publication. The answer is C.
